title: vulpia murorum
intro: Vulpia murorum, also known as Wood Millet or Brome, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Vulpia murorum include soothing stomach ulcers and diarrhea, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Vulpia murorum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
